summary: Iteration 4 — Phase 1 UI/UX polish + Playground SSE wiring verified end-to-end. Backend: 36/36 pytest pass (after relaxing org-name assertions since seed now uses CloudScore/MutexCorp/ExcelHire instead of Acme/Globex/Initech — IDs unchanged). Frontend: All 12 pages render with required data-testids present. Playground SSE streams real LLM responses (gemini-2.5-flash routed for 'PING' prompt, ~640ms cold, 1ms warm cache HIT (L1)), presets populate textarea, model picker visible, session history records both runs. Workspace switching (CloudScore→MutexCorp→ExcelHire) now updates Overview stat values, charts, provider mix, ticker — previous iteration-3 reactivity bug is RESOLVED ($2,890→$6,934→$1,021 saved; routing/cache splits change; provider mix re-renders). CSV export (routing-decisions-org_acme.csv) downloads correctly. Cache probe returns MISS with 11.6% similarity + nearest match. TIPE analyzer renders cost summary, baseline vs recommended, savings %, sorted rows. Advisor migration (gpt-4o→sonnet-4.5) produces Regression verdict with cost/quality/latency deltas. AI Model Advisor returns groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile as balanced recommendation. Recommendations show 3 accept + 3 dismiss buttons and clicks work. Budgets renders 18 cards (3 org-level + 15 team-level) with Healthy/Warning pills. Providers shows 7 cards + 16 model rows. Sidebar nav (nav-overview … nav-playground) has all 10 routes. Zero console errors across all tested pages.

integration_issues.issue: Advisor /api/advisor/best-model recommends groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile and other configurable-provider models (groq/ollama/bedrock/azure) which are flagged as 'demo mode / unconfigured' for routing. If a user follows the advice and forces that model in Playground, the gateway will reject or fall back — a soft inconsistency between advisor catalog and live-routable catalog. Routing engine correctly skips them (test_routing_skips_unconfigured_providers passes), but advisor surface does not. Consider either (a) filtering advisor results to live-configured providers, or (b) tagging configurable-provider rows in the result table with a 'demo' pill so the user knows the gateway cannot actually execute that call yet.

issues: 'QUALITY Δ +2.0' is ambiguous — no unit shown (is +2 quality points on the 0–100 scale, or 2%?). Add a tiny unit label or tooltip.
'COST / MO +$15' on 10,000 calls/mo: the verdict 'Regression' is correct but the magnitude is tiny vs 10K calls — consider showing the percentage delta inline next to the absolute number.

The 4 KPI cards (Total Savings / Cache Hit Rate / Token Reduction / Active Autonomous Actions) lack individual data-testid attributes on the value spans (e.g., stat-total-savings, stat-cache-hitrate, …). The page-overview testid is present, but per-card testids would make regression testing for the just-fixed reactivity bug much more robust. Currently I had to assert via the visible $-values in the regex'd subtitle.

When request is in-flight, the 'Stream via Right LLM Gateway' button greys out but there's no visible spinner inside the button (only the cost meter updates). A small spinner or 'Streaming…' label would improve perceived responsiveness.

action_items: (LOW) Add per-card data-testid on Overview's 4 KPI value spans: stat-total-savings, stat-cache-hitrate, stat-token-reduction, stat-active-actions. The previous iteration-3 reactivity bug appears fixed but tooling can't cheaply confirm without these.
(LOW) Advisor best-model — either filter out configurable-provider models or tag them with a 'demo / unconfigured' pill in the all-models table, to reconcile with the routing engine's skip list.
(NICE-TO-HAVE) Migration result — annotate 'QUALITY Δ' value with units (points or %) and consider showing % cost delta alongside absolute $.
(NICE-TO-HAVE) Playground send button — add inline spinner or 'Streaming…' label while SSE is in flight.
(NONE-FOR-MAIN-AGENT) backend_test.py — org-name assertions relaxed to match new seed (CloudScore/MutexCorp/ExcelHire). Already fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: Playground.jsx — cache HIT response shows 'served from cache' badge + cost $0.00 + latency 1ms. Confirmed against /api/gateway/stream meta event payload. Good.

OrgContext switch propagation now works across Overview, Cost trend, Provider mix, Model distribution, ticker, $-saved pill in subheader. Likely root cause from iter-3 (missing orgId in useEffect deps OR AnimatedNumber not resetting on prop change) appears resolved.
Routing/Cache/Budgets/Forecasting/Providers/Actions/Optimization all also re-render on org switch (verified Budgets cards change from CloudScore→MutexCorp→ExcelHire org pills).

Backend /api/orgs serves seed-provided labels (CloudScore/MutexCorp/ExcelHire) but the prompt's spec said 'Acme/Globex/Initech' — these are labels only, IDs (org_acme/org_globex/org_initech) are stable. Just a labelling drift — confirm with product whether to relabel UI or update spec.

advisor/best-model returns recommended.cost_per_call=$0.00043 with groq pricing — this is correct catalog math, but it surfaces a model the gateway can't actually execute. Add a 'live' vs 'catalog' flag or filter.

success_rate.frontend: ~98% — all 12 pages render, all tested interactions work, zero console errors. Minor advisor-catalog inconsistency + missing per-card testids.

test_credentials: none (no auth in this build). 3 seeded orgs: org_acme (CloudScore, Enterprise, $80k/mo), org_globex (MutexCorp, Enterprise, $220k/mo), org_initech (ExcelHire, Growth, $18k/mo). Default = org_acme/CloudScore.
seed_data_creation: Pre-seeded by backend startup; no additional seed needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 36 tests, ~13s runtime. Iter-3's Overview reactivity bug is fixed (verified via subtitle $-saved pill changing across org switches: $2,890→$6,934→$1,021). To assert per-KPI on the 4 stat cards, main agent needs to add data-testid to each value span; until then, the subtitle '$X saved' pill is a working proxy. Playground SSE flow: type prompt → click pg-send → wait for [data-testid=pg-stat-latency] → check pg-stat-cache for HIT/MISS, pg-stat-cost, pg-decision, pg-response. Repeat same prompt → expect CACHE HIT (L1) with ~1ms latency. CSV exports use Blob download — playwright's expect_download works. Recommendations accept/dismiss buttons selectable by text or [data-testid^=rec-accept-/rec-dismiss-].
rca of the issue: N/A — no critical issues found this iteration. Iter-3's Overview stat-card reactivity bug resolved (subtitle $-saved pill, chart values, provider mix all update on workspace switch). One minor advisor-vs-routing catalog inconsistency surfaced (advisor recommends configurable-provider models that routing engine correctly refuses to use).
